articles.title: OpenAI and Broadcom's Jalapeño Chip
articles.summary: OpenAI and Broadcom's Jalapeño chip is designed for inference, the compute-intensive process of serving AI models to users. Jalapeño is the first in a planned family of LLM inference accelerators optimized for performance per watt.

articles.title: Tesla and Sunrun Announce 16GW Virtual Power Plant
articles.summary: Sunrun, Tesla, and Renew Home have announced an agreement to aggregate more than 16 gigawatts of home batteries and solar capacity into virtual power plants that can sell electricity back to the grid, including for AI data centers.

articles.title: Intercept: Making Respiratory Infections History
articles.summary: Intercept is a new $500 million initiative aimed at making respiratory infections like the common cold and flu a thing of the past. The project plans to develop universal vaccines and treatments for a broad range of respiratory viruses.

articles.title: The Case for Language-Native Software
articles.summary: Natural language is becoming an interface. Software is beginning to understand instructions in plain English, and the author argues that leaning into this shift — rather than fighting it — unlocks a new paradigm for building software.

articles.title: Amazon Is Selling Software That Does White-Collar Work
articles.summary: Amazon is increasingly in the business of selling software that does white-collar work. However, AWS CEO Matt Garman argues that jobs won't disappear — they'll shift, with developers becoming more like managers of AI systems.

articles.title: Dario Amodei Reportedly Difficult in White House Negotiations
articles.summary: Dario Amodei was reportedly weird and hard to deal with during the negotiations between the White House and AI labs over compute export controls and safety commitments.

articles.title: Stop Programming in Markdown
articles.summary: People are using LLMs inappropriately in situations where regular code would fare much better. The author argues that using natural language as a specification language for complex systems creates fragile, hard-to-maintain software.

articles.title: Qualcomm Announces Dragonfly C1000 Server Chip
articles.summary: The Dragonfly C1000 is a general-purpose server processor designed to compete in AI data center workloads. Expected to be available in 2028, it targets a market dominated by Nvidia and AMD.

articles.title: Middlemen on Messaging Apps
articles.summary: Middlemen on messaging apps like Telegram, WeChat, and WhatsApp are offering access to people and services in exchange for fees, creating a shadow economy as messaging apps become infrastructure for commerce in many parts of the world.

articles.title: Writing Loops, Not Prompts
articles.summary: Automate the parts of prompting that you keep repeating, and keep judgment close to the parts that require it. Loops compound; prompts don't. The author shows how building small automated feedback cycles outperforms one-shot prompting.

articles.title: Build Agents That Open PRs
articles.summary: A tutorial on building AI coding agents that automate the creation of GitHub pull requests, reducing developer toil on routine code changes.

articles.title: Lessons Learned Rewriting PostHog's SQL Parser
articles.summary: Lessons learned when rewriting PostHog's SQL parser using multiple Claude Code sessions running in parallel. The rewrite achieved a massive speed improvement and reduced parser complexity significantly.

articles.title: OpenAI and Broadcom Unveil Jalapeño Chip
articles.summary: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño, the first accelerator in a planned family of LLM inference chips optimized for performance per watt. The chip is designed specifically for the inference workloads that dominate AI deployment costs.

articles.title: AI Researchers Continue to Leave Google for Its Rivals
articles.summary: Bloomberg reported that Gemini researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel left Google for Anthropic, continuing a trend of top AI talent departures from the search giant. The departures underscore the competitive pressure Google faces in retaining researchers who can command significant packages elsewhere.

articles.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash Gets Native Computer Use
articles.summary: Google launched native computer-use capabilities for Gemini 3.5 Flash, allowing the lightweight model to interact with computer interfaces directly. The feature enables Gemini to complete agentic tasks by controlling browsers and desktop applications.

articles.title: Notes on Amazon and Perplexity
articles.summary: Amazon is suing Perplexity for breaking the Amazon Store's Conditions of Use as Perplexity's Comet browser circumvented Amazon's anti-scraping protections. The lawsuit highlights growing tension between AI-powered browsers that reformat the web and the platforms that depend on controlling their interfaces.

articles.title: GLM-5.2 Is the Step Change for Open Agents
articles.summary: GLM-5.2 seemed like an incremental update, but the small change in benchmarks and training approaches opens the door for real open-source agent systems that can match proprietary alternatives on agentic tasks.

articles.title: Nvidia Launches NeMo AutoModel on Hugging Face
articles.summary: NVIDIA launched NeMo AutoModel on Hugging Face to optimize the fine-tuning pipelines of massive AI models. The tool automates hyperparameter selection and training configuration, reducing the expertise required to fine-tune large language models.

articles.title: FLAT: Triangle Splats From Video Diffusion Latents
articles.summary: Google's FLAT introduces a feedforward method that decodes triangle splats directly from video diffusion latents, enabling fast 3D reconstruction from video without per-scene optimization.

articles.title: Qwen-AgentWorld
articles.summary: Alibaba introduced Qwen-AgentWorld, a family of language world models trained on more than 100 million agent trajectories. The models are designed to simulate environment dynamics and serve as world models for training AI agents.

articles.title: Orca: AI Coding Agent
articles.summary: An open-source AI coding agent by Stably AI that assists with software development tasks directly in the terminal.

articles.title: Amazon's Nuclear Energy Advantage
articles.summary: Amazon has an incumbent advantage in the race for hyperscalers to get their hands on more electricity from nuclear sources, thanks to early deals and existing relationships with nuclear operators that competitors are now scrambling to replicate.

articles.title: Anthropic and Alibaba Collaborate on Distillation
articles.summary: Anthropic and Alibaba have initiated a collaborative open-source framework focused on distilling large AI models into smaller, more efficient versions that can run on commodity hardware without sacrificing significant capability.

articles.title: Claude Code Hints at Memory Return
articles.summary: Claude Code v2.2.190 introduces several string changes that hint at preparations for the return of memory features. The changes suggest Anthropic is working on persistent memory capabilities that would allow Claude Code to remember context across sessions.

articles.title: Mirendil Raises $200M to Fund Own AI Research
articles.summary: Mirendil has raised $200 million in seed funding to make and distribute AI that accelerates AI research itself. The company's thesis is that AI can dramatically compress the timeline for fundamental AI discoveries.

articles.title: GPT-5.5 Instant Rollout
articles.summary: OpenAI has initiated the rollout of an upgraded GPT-5.5 Instant model directly inside ChatGPT, offering faster response times and improved performance on everyday tasks compared to the previous Instant tier.

articles.title: Perplexity Computer for Counsel
articles.summary: Perplexity launched Computer for Counsel, an AI-driven legal operations tool designed to automate legal research and document workflows for law firms and corporate legal departments.

articles.title: Fortibleed Credential Harvesting Campaign
articles.summary: A Russian-speaking initial access broker has run FortiBleed since February, brute-forcing over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls to harvest credentials. The campaign targets unpatched devices and sells access to ransomware groups.

articles.title: Zafran Discloses DifyTap Vulnerabilities in Dify
articles.summary: Zafran Security disclosed DifyTap, four flaws in the open-source LLMOps platform Dify. The vulnerabilities could allow attackers to compromise AI pipelines built on the platform and exfiltrate model data.

articles.title: ModeloRAT and Mistic Backdoor
articles.summary: Symantec and Zscaler tie the Python-based ModeloRAT, delivered via WinPython and run via a signed Python binary, to a suspected Chinese APT. The backdoor uses the Mistic framework for command and control evasion.

articles.title: Cordyceps Malware
articles.summary: A technical analysis of the Cordyceps malware family, detailing its infection mechanisms and persistence techniques used in recent enterprise intrusions.

articles.title: J&J Web App Hacks
articles.summary: A detailed breakdown of security vulnerabilities discovered in Johnson & Johnson web applications, covering the research methodology and disclosure process.

articles.title: Detecting Misuse With Claude Compliance API
articles.summary: In a previous post in this series, the author introduced an SDK to ingest Claude Compliance API events into a SIEM. This post extends that work to build detection rules for identifying misuse patterns in Claude API traffic.

articles.title: Anthropic's Mythos Model Finds Government Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: A US official says Anthropic's Mythos model, tested with intelligence agencies under a classified project, was able to identify cybersecurity vulnerabilities in government systems at a rate that impressed analysts.

articles.title: How AI Is Rewriting the SecOps Playbook
articles.summary: As AI compresses the gap between vulnerability disclosure and exploitation to minutes, the SecOps playbook must change. Wiz outlines how security operations teams need to rethink detection, response, and remediation timelines.

articles.title: Meta Pauses Employee Monitoring Initiative
articles.summary: Meta halted its April Model Compatibility Initiative, which captured employee keystrokes, screen activity, and browsing behavior. The pause came after internal backlash and questions about the legality of the data collection program.

articles.title: NSA Employees Lose Access to Anthropic's Mythos 5
articles.summary: Parts of the NSA lost access to Anthropic's Mythos 5 after the Trump administration imposed export controls on advanced AI models. The restrictions complicate ongoing efforts to use commercial AI tools for signals intelligence work.

articles.title: Rewriting PostHog's SQL Parser With AI
articles.summary: A new SQL parser for PostHog was developed using AI, with a huge speed improvement compared to the previous system. The author describes using Claude Code to generate and iterate on parser code, significantly cutting development time.

articles.title: Linear Moves From styled-components to StyleX
articles.summary: Linear is transitioning from styled-components to StyleX for its React apps primarily to improve performance. The post covers the migration strategy and the measurable gains in bundle size and runtime CSS application.

articles.title: Lies, Damn Lies, and Database Benchmarks
articles.summary: QuestDB is an open-source time-series database designed for high-performance applications, known for pushing benchmarks. This post unpacks how database benchmarks can mislead, covering the common tricks vendors use to inflate numbers.

articles.title: Stealing Is a Skill
articles.summary: Stealing, or creatively borrowing from others, is a skill. Learning from others' creations can improve one's own perspective, and the best designers and engineers systematically study the work they admire.

articles.title: An Opinionated and Mainly Correct Guide
articles.summary: An opinionated guide to software engineering practices, covering code organization, team dynamics, and how to make decisions that hold up over time.

articles.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash Computer Use
articles.summary: Gemini 3.5 Flash now has integrated computer use capabilities that improve agentic tasks and enable developers to automate browser workflows. The feature is available via the Gemini API and is designed to be efficient enough for production use.

articles.title: Prompt Injection Explained
articles.summary: Prompt injection is a vulnerability in LLMs coming from their inability to effectively distinguish between instructions and data. The post gives a mechanistic explanation of why current architectures are fundamentally susceptible to this attack.

articles.title: Why Big AI Labs Are Hiring So Many Philosophers
articles.summary: Major AI labs are increasingly hiring philosophers to address the complex ethical and operational challenges inherent in AI development. The piece examines what philosophers actually do at these companies and whether it changes the products they ship.

articles.title: How to Apply Professional Design Principles in AI Apps
articles.summary: In AI app development, applying professional design principles is crucial for creating visually appealing and user-friendly interfaces. The post covers typography, spacing, hierarchy, and how to handle AI's inherently unpredictable output within a polished UI.

articles.title: The HTTP QUERY Method
articles.summary: The newly proposed HTTP QUERY method is a safe and idempotent request method allowing clients to make complex queries with a request body. It fills a gap in HTTP semantics that previously forced developers to use POST for read-only search operations.

articles.title: The Invention of Spell Checkers
articles.summary: Tony Krueger, who improved spelling and grammar checking in word processors by introducing the red underline feature, shares the history of how real-time spell checking was invented and how it became universal in software.

articles.title: The Hidden Burnout Crisis Facing Social Media Marketers
articles.summary: More than 40% of social media marketers plan to leave their jobs within two years, and nearly half say they get little supervisor support. The research points to always-on expectations and the emotional labor of managing brand presence as primary drivers.

articles.title: How Snapchat Builds Brands
articles.summary: Meaningful engagement matters more than raw reach. Snapchat's research found that users who view their time on a platform as quality time engage more deeply with ads and develop stronger brand recall than users on platforms they feel are wasting their time.

articles.title: Gamify Your Product Promotions
articles.summary: Gamified promotions can increase purchase intent for fun and hedonic products by turning discounts into a playful challenge. The research shows that effort-based rewards feel more earned and drive stronger positive brand associations.

articles.title: Reach Is Not a Vanity Metric
articles.summary: The 'reach is a vanity metric' idea is challenged here. Reach matters when it's intentional and tied to funnel design — broad reach to the right audience at the top of the funnel drives compounding returns that engagement metrics alone can't capture.

articles.title: You Don't Need to Spend Hours on a Weekly Email
articles.summary: A short weekly email can be written in 15 minutes by curating 3-5 of the best social posts from the week. The post makes the case that consistency beats production value for most newsletters, and that over-engineering your email is a reason people don't start.

articles.title: Vibe Coding Marketing Report
articles.summary: AI code generation has gone mainstream in marketing fast. 41% of marketers say it's already embedded in their workflows, primarily for building landing pages, internal tools, and campaign automations without engineering support.

articles.title: Martech Spend Hits 5-Year Low
articles.summary: Martech spending hit a 5-year low as a share of marketing budgets in 2026, dropping to 19% from previous highs. CMOs are consolidating tools rather than expanding stacks, with AI features folded into existing platforms reducing the case for new point solutions.

articles.title: AI Fatigue in Consumers
articles.summary: 65% of consumers wish brands would stop mentioning AI in their marketing, and 63% say they'd be less likely to trust AI-generated ads. The Harris Poll findings suggest AI as a marketing message has peaked and is now actively hurting brand perception for many audiences.

articles.title: Warner Bros. Unveils New Animation Logo
articles.summary: Warner Bros. unveiled a new flat-design Animation logo at Annecy, featuring a simplified WB shield and Tweety flying alongside it. The redesign modernizes the brand while retaining enough nostalgia to feel familiar to audiences who grew up with the classic logo.

articles.title: Foldable iPhone Ultra Back on Track for September
articles.summary: Reports suggest Apple's foldable iPhone Ultra is back on track for a September unveiling after resolving 3D-printed hinge problems that had threatened to delay mass production.

articles.title: Adobe Launches Firefly Graph for Creative Cloud
articles.summary: Adobe has launched Firefly Graph for Creative Cloud customers, a node-based workflow tool that connects creative assets with AI generation steps. It lets designers build repeatable production pipelines that combine Photoshop, Illustrator, and Firefly in automated sequences.

articles.title: My Beef With Agentic Design Systems
articles.summary: A critique of the emerging wave of AI-driven design systems that claim to generate and maintain components autonomously, arguing they create more fragility than they solve.

articles.title: Organising Documentation for Humans and AI
articles.summary: As AI agents increasingly read and act on documentation alongside humans, the structure, naming conventions, and metadata of docs need to serve two very different audiences at once.

articles.title: How Accepting 'Just Build This Thing' Changed Design
articles.summary: As AI tools make it easier for stakeholders to arrive with polished solutions already in hand, designers risk being sidelined. The author argues for a new kind of design advocacy that engages earlier and reframes the designer's role around judgment rather than execution.

articles.title: A Consistency of Excellence
articles.summary: Modern design systems have made visual consistency an end in itself, sacrificing the individual excellence of components. The author argues that the best design work achieves a different kind of consistency — one built from the inside out through shared craft values rather than shared tokens.

articles.title: Designing With AI: Why Claude Design Is Not the Future of Enterprise
articles.summary: Claude Design accelerates early-stage UI generation but lacks the depth enterprise teams need for production work. The post evaluates where AI design tools currently fall short on component consistency, design token handling, and handoff quality.

articles.title: Studio Gruhl's Identity for Rerun
articles.summary: Studio Gruhl created a new identity for Rerun by rejecting the polished, futuristic aesthetic typically associated with tech brands. The result is a deliberately rough and human visual language that communicates authenticity in a space saturated with sameness.

articles.title: Moving Beyond UX: The Rise of Agentic Experience Design
articles.summary: Agentic Experience (AX) Design is an emerging discipline focused not on human-facing interfaces, but on designing for AI agents acting on behalf of users. The post outlines what AX designers need to think about: agent goals, failure modes, trust signals, and handoff points.

articles.title: The Artist Perfectly Reimagining Movie Scenes Through Art History
articles.summary: Pop culture meets art history in the work of Norro Bey, who blends iconic movie scenes with classical painting styles. The series reimagines moments from blockbusters through the visual language of Renaissance, Baroque, and Impressionist masters.

articles.title: Stablecore Launches Stablecoin Program for Credit Unions
articles.summary: The programme is put together by Stablecore, a platform that helps community banks and credit unions offer stablecoins, tokenised deposits, and digital asset products, and Circuit, a research and development Credit Union Service Organisation.

articles.title: Mastercard and PrivatBank Complete Ukraine's First AI Agent Payment
articles.summary: Agentic commerce — where AI-powered agents act on behalf of consumers to shop, manage subscriptions, and complete everyday tasks — is poised to transform the way Ukrainians shop online. By enabling fully recognized and authenticated agentic transactions, Mastercard's agentic framework brings AI agents into the payment flow as visible, governed participants, ensuring that every transaction is secure, transparent, and trusted.

articles.title: Modern Treasury Partners Sardine to Detect Fraud Earlier
articles.summary: Modern Treasury today announced a partnership with Sardine to enhance transaction monitoring and fraud prevention capabilities for businesses moving money across the US and globally.

articles.title: SpaceX S-1 Full Analysis
articles.summary: This analysis breaks down SpaceX's revised S-1, focusing on major AI compute deals with Anthropic, Google, and Reflection AI, including implied GPU lease rates, power capacity, and revenue per gigawatt. The piece also examines SpaceX's IPO float dynamics, arguing that limited initial supply, index demand, and staged lockup expirations could create a near-term squeeze followed by later digestion as more shares unlock.

articles.title: UPI, PIX, and the Rise of Public Payment Rails
articles.summary: For many years, digital payment innovation was associated mainly with private networks, card schemes, wallets, and technology platforms. That story is still important, but it is no longer complete — some of the most significant recent payment developments have come from public or public-supported payment rails like UPI in India and PIX in Brazil.

articles.title: Stripe Directory Public Preview
articles.summary: Stripe Directory is a searchable network directory that lets developers and AI agents discover businesses, services, Stripe Apps, and machine payment endpoints. The product positions Stripe as infrastructure for agentic commerce by allowing agents to search for providers, parse structured results, and programmatically pay for services through machine payment rails.

articles.title: Thredd Rolls Out Visa Cloud Connect in Asia Pacific
articles.summary: Thredd, an AI-first issuer processing platform, announced the implementation of Visa Cloud Connect in the Asia Pacific, marking a significant regional milestone in the company's broader cloud transformation strategy.

articles.title: Visa, Mastercard Fend Off Fee Foes
articles.summary: Visa and Mastercard scored a series of wins against merchant efforts to curb card fees, as state-level fee restrictions in Colorado and Illinois stalled and a federal court approved a contested merchant settlement. The long-running fight over swipe fees will continue, but the card networks have largely maintained their grip.

articles.title: NYSE Parent ICE and OKX Form Tokenized Equities Joint Venture
articles.summary: Intercontinental Exchange and OKX are forming a 50-50 joint venture to build a regulated US broker-dealer and futures commission merchant for tokenized NYSE equities and ICE futures, pending regulatory approval.

articles.title: Polymarket Reportedly Paid Creators to Post Deceptive Videos About Fake Bets
articles.summary: Polymarket reportedly paid online creators to post promotional videos showing fake trades and winnings on near-identical copies of its prediction market site, according to a Wall Street Journal investigation.

articles.title: Meta Taps New WhatsApp Boss in $900M Bet on India's Cred
articles.summary: Meta is investing $900 million in Indian fintech Cred for a roughly 20% stake while appointing Cred founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp. The deal signals Meta's intent to expand WhatsApp payments and financial services in the world's most populous country.

articles.title: Ethereum Foundation Cuts 54 Staff in Largest Restructuring
articles.summary: The Ethereum Foundation cut 54 staff on June 23, representing roughly 20% of the organization and concluding a months-long restructuring process tied to its newly adopted Mandate and Treasury Management Policy.

articles.title: Bitcoin Slips Toward $63,000 as Tech Selloff Drags Risk Assets Lower
articles.summary: A rotation out of chip and AI-focused equities pulled Bitcoin to $62,840 (-1.1% daily, -3.5% weekly) and Ether to $1,719, as South Korea's Kospi fell over 6% and Nasdaq 100 futures dropped 1.3%, reinforcing crypto's tightening correlation with the tech trade.

articles.title: 0x Opens Swap API to AI Agents Paying $0.01 per Request in USDC
articles.summary: 0x Protocol opened its Swap API to AI agents charging $0.01 per request in USDC, paid directly from agent-controlled wallets with no API key required. The integration runs on the x402 standard (HTTP 402 Payment Required) with Alchemy AgentPay providing payment rails.

articles.title: Synthetix Governance Votes to Retire sUSD, Pay Holders in Vested SNX
articles.summary: Synthetix governance approved SIP-423 to freeze the sUSD stablecoin contract and retire it entirely, compensating holders at 4 SNX per sUSD in vested tokens rather than a dollar-pegged alternative.

articles.title: The Case for an AI-Exposure Investment Product
articles.summary: There is no investment product that gives regular people true asymmetric exposure to AI — current options top out at Nvidia and AI ETFs, with pre-IPO, early-stage venture, and sovereign fund allocations gated behind wealth and access.

articles.title: David Marcus on Lightspark Reaching Escape Velocity in Global Payments
articles.summary: Lightspark CEO David Marcus reflects on four years of methodically building payment infrastructure, describing the company as having finally reached the sweet spot where capabilities, licensing, and partnerships converge to enable global real-time payments at scale.

articles.title: Taiko Halts Its Network After a Bridge Exploit
articles.summary: Taiko halted its Ethereum L2 on June 22 after an attacker forged cross-chain withdrawal proofs to drain approximately $1.7 million from the bridge and token vault.

articles.title: Tom Lee Says 'Best Years for Crypto Remain Ahead' as Bitmine Buys 52,203 ETH
articles.summary: Bitmine Immersion Technologies acquired 52,203 ETH last week, bringing its treasury to 5.67 million ETH representing 4.7% of total ether supply, valued at $9.8 billion. Fundstrat's Tom Lee argues the accumulation reflects a broader institutional conviction that crypto's best returns are still ahead.

articles.title: Xapo Bank Updates BTC-Backed Loans
articles.summary: Xapo Bank announced an updated Bitcoin-backed loan product allowing members to unlock up to $5 million in USD liquidity instantly while keeping their BTC, with improved loan-to-value ratios and automated margin call management.

articles.title: Chinese Bitcoin Miner Jiang Zhuoer Calls BTC Bear Market Bottom
articles.summary: Jiang Zhuoer, one of China's most prominent Bitcoin miners, predicts the current bear market will bottom between October and December in the $42,000–$44,000 range, citing miner profitability curves and historical halving cycle patterns.

articles.title: ETH Exchange Reserves Hit All-Time Low
articles.summary: ETH exchange reserves have fallen to an all-time low, a confluence of supply and development signals that analyst aixbt argues points to reduced sell pressure and a structurally tighter market for ether.

articles.title: Tokenized Stocks Are a Net Positive for Crypto, Not a Threat
articles.summary: Bringing tokenized equities onchain raises the quality bar for crypto tokens, drives more onchain activity, and introduces more people to crypto's core value proposition of programmable, permissionless ownership.

articles.title: Anthropic Reimagines Claude in Slack as Always-On Agentic AI Coworker
articles.summary: Anthropic is retiring its existing Claude in Slack connector in favor of an always-on, agentic Slack coworker called Claude Tag. Claude Tag can join Slack organizations as a shared team member, learn from channel context and permitted data sources, and be delegated tasks via @Claude, with options like ambient updates and scheduled multi-hour or multi-day work.

articles.title: Qualcomm Buys Modular to Challenge Nvidia's AI Software Moat
articles.summary: Qualcomm is buying Modular for about $3.9B to strengthen its AI software layer across data centers, edge environments, and heterogeneous hardware. Modular gives Qualcomm a better shot at competing with Nvidia's CUDA ecosystem by helping AI workloads run across different chips without teams rewriting software for every processor.

articles.title: AI Coding Costs Could Outrun Developer Salaries
articles.summary: Gartner says AI coding could become more expensive than the average developer salary by 2028 as token-based pricing overtakes flat subscriptions and usage grows across coding tools, agents, cloud platforms, and team experiments. The takeaway for IT leaders is that AI needs FinOps-style governance: usage tracking, ownership, throttling, and clear business value before costs quietly compound.

articles.title: Data Lakehouses Are Becoming the Foundation for Enterprise AI
articles.summary: Data lakehouses are becoming the enterprise foundation for AI because they combine broad data access with governance, security, auditability, and structure. Agentic AI makes the data layer much more strategic — companies now need semantic context, agent identities, access controls, and audit trails before letting AI systems autonomously query business data.

articles.title: Google Apps Script Becomes a Core Workspace Service
articles.summary: Google Apps Script is now an official Google Workspace core service, meaning it gets enterprise-grade data protection, admin controls, standard technical support, and Workspace terms coverage.

articles.title: Bunny Makes DNS Free to Pull More Apps Onto Its Edge Platform
articles.summary: Bunny.net is eliminating DNS query fees and offering free DNS hosting for up to 500 domains per account, with no query limits, no per-request billing, and features like smart records and health monitoring included.

articles.title: New Site Calls Out Companies Without Passkey Support
articles.summary: A new site from security researcher Scott Helme tracks major apps and services that still do not offer passkey login, including Instagram, Netflix, and Spotify.

articles.title: Cisco Adds AI Troubleshooting for Industrial Networks
articles.summary: Cisco is introducing AI Troubleshooting for Industrial Networks, an always-on agent in Cisco Cloud Control that monitors factory-floor network signals, clusters related alerts, diagnoses root causes, and recommends fixes for OT teams.

articles.title: Jamf Highlights DDM for Real-Time Mac Fleet Visibility
articles.summary: Apple's Declarative Device Management gives IT teams faster Mac fleet visibility by letting devices proactively report status changes instead of waiting for traditional MDM command polling.

articles.title: Superhuman Buys GPTZero to Add an AI Authenticity Layer
articles.summary: Superhuman, formerly Grammarly, is acquiring GPTZero to add AI detection, plagiarism checks, citation verification, source validation, and writing-origin tools into its productivity platform.

articles.title: Google Meet Hardware Adds Room Codes in Early Preview
articles.summary: Google Meet hardware admins can join the early preview for room codes, letting users join meetings from conference rooms with a short code instead of manually finding the meeting invite or calendar event.

articles.title: How Netflix Simplified Batch Compute With Kueue
articles.summary: Netflix simplified its batch compute platform by replacing its Compute Managed Batch system with Kueue, a Kubernetes-native job queuing and scheduling system. The migration was done by maintaining full API compatibility, converting their tenant hierarchy into Kueue Cohorts and ClusterQueues, and adding powerful features like preemption-based fair sharing.

articles.title: Inside One Engineer's Journey to Master Long-Running Agents
articles.summary: Long-running coding agents work better when they are grounded in structured context and persistent artifacts: repo research, knowledge bases, plans, progress files, and review notes. DoorDash's Agentic Orchestrator turns those handoffs into a state-machine workflow, giving agents enough shared state to tackle bigger tasks without losing coherence.

articles.title: Real-Time Personalisation at Scale: How Zepto Understands What You Want, Right Now
articles.summary: Zepto built a Dual Sequence ReRanker for real-time personalisation that combines a user's long-term history with their current in-session behavior using separate transformer encoders, target-aware pooling, a learned fusion gate, and real-time signals.

articles.title: Client-Side Load Balancing at a Million Requests per Second
articles.summary: Zalando built a high-performance client-side load balancer in-process for their Product Read API to handle over a million requests per second of internal fan-out traffic, implementing consistent hashing, Kubernetes watch-based discovery, N-ring fade-in for smooth scale-ups, and AZ-aware routing.

articles.title: Broken Windows of Data
articles.summary: Data warehouse quality needs to be built into the full development lifecycle, not left to final review. Teams can stop small inconsistencies from spreading into shared definitions, dashboards, and business logic by shifting checks earlier through modeling reviews, local validation, CI/CD, AI-assisted review, and monitoring.

articles.title: Why Technically Excellent Data Teams Still Fail
articles.summary: Many technically excellent data teams remain irrelevant because strong execution is no longer enough. The real value comes from moving beyond just delivering data to actively driving decisions through opinionated analysis and influencing business outcomes.

articles.title: Can We Agree on a Storage/Workload Architecture Taxonomy?
articles.summary: Data storage architectures (OLTP, OLAP, HTAP, and LTAP) can be classified into a taxonomy evaluated by system count, workloads, data visibility, and durable copies. This framework organizes modern data systems into Single Tier, Internal Tiering, Hybrid, Materializing, and Shared Tiering models.

articles.title: SQL Concepts Lab
articles.summary: SQL Concepts Lab is an interactive, browser-based tutorial built with DuckDB-WASM that lets you learn and experiment with core SQL concepts directly in your browser without any setup.

articles.title: dbtrail
articles.summary: dbtrail gives MySQL point-in-time recovery by streaming binlogs and indexing row changes with before-and-after images. Teams can inspect changes, create reversal SQL, browse history, or run time-travel queries, with MCP support for AI-assisted recovery.

articles.title: SQLBuild
articles.summary: SQLBuild adds change-aware execution to dbt by fingerprinting SQL transformations and only rerunning models whose logic has actually changed, reducing dbt run times significantly for large data warehouses.
